shipped.description: Git-native prompt regression testing with judge calibration for LLM CI/CD pipelines
shipped.pypi_status: READY_TO_PUBLISH — needs PyPI account + OIDC trusted publishing setup
shipped.key_differentiator: Judge calibration — verifies LLM judge agrees with humans before trusting it as a CI gate.
shipped.next_step: Push to GitHub repo buildworld-ai/prompt-lock, publish to PyPI, launch HN + Dev.to + r/LocalLLaMA

shipped.description: Behavioral contract enforcement for LLM function calls — @contract decorator + versioned behavioral specs + drift detection
shipped.key_differentiator: The only pip-installable library that defines, versions, and enforces behavioral (not just structural) contracts on LLM function calls, with SQLite drift logging and CI gate.
shipped.bibleworld_pattern: PAT-035 (Acts 2:1-13 — Pentecost: many languages, one coherent message contract)
shipped.competitive_status: GREEN — confirmed no direct competitor across 4 live searches (March 2026).
shipped.next_step: Push to GitHub repo buildworld-ai/llm-contract, publish to PyPI, launch HN + Dev.to

shipped.description: Git-native PR semantic intent verifier — checks whether a PR's code changes actually fulfill the stated intent in the description. Catches silent contract violations before they hit production.
shipped.pypi_status: READY_TO_PUBLISH — needs PyPI account + OIDC trusted publishing setup
shipped.key_differentiator: The only open-source tool that verifies whether a PR's code changes fulfill its stated intent — pre-merge, with a CI gate, for any git repo. CodeRabbit/PR-Agent do code review; drift-guard does intent verification.
shipped.bibleworld_pattern: PAT-036 (Romans 7:7 — The Law makes violations visible)
shipped.competitive_status: GREEN — 5 live searches March 2026 confirmed no direct competitor. 'drift-guard' (hyphenated) name available on PyPI. driftguard (no hyphen) is ML data drift detection — completely different domain.
shipped.next_step: Push to GitHub repo buildworld-ai/drift-guard, publish to PyPI, launch HN + Dev.to

revenue_status.note: Three tools MIT OSS — code-complete, PyPI-ready. Revenue path: enterprise support + hosted dashboard (v0.3+). Traction gate: 100 GitHub stars + 100 PyPI installs per tool.
revenue_status.law_2_trigger: If all three tools under 100 installs after 3 weeks of launch, pivot to GrantPilot (direct $29-499/month revenue, 2-3 week MVP).
revenue_status.blocker: PyPI account + GitHub repos needed — manual step outside build loop.

currently_building.status: ALL THREE TOOLS CODE-COMPLETE — waiting for PyPI/GitHub deploy
currently_building.next_build: cot-coherence v0.1.0 (BibleWorld BUILD FOURTH, score 8.00) OR pivot to GrantPilot

cycle_3_summary.competitive_verdict: GREEN — PR semantic intent verification space is unoccupied. CodeRabbit/PR-Agent do code review, not intent verification. 'drift-guard' PyPI name available.
cycle_3_summary.buildworld_additions: Updated to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (latest), hatchling build backend (BuildWorld standard), 7 additional tests (41 total vs 30 in prototype), Python 3.10+ minimum

competitive_intelligence.pr_semantic_intent_verification: GREEN — No direct competitor confirmed in 3 fresh searches March 2026. semantic-prs checks commit message FORMAT (conventional commits) not intent. CodeRabbit/PR-Agent suggest improvements, do not verify intent.
competitive_intelligence.prompt_regression_testing: GREEN — prompt-lock still unoccupied as standalone pip package
competitive_intelligence.coderabbit_pricing: $24-30/dev/month. Qodo/PR-Agent $19-30/dev/month. drift-guard is free MIT OSS — massive GTM wedge.
competitive_intelligence.driftguard_no_hyphen: ML data drift detection library (v0.1.6, GitHub: kiplangatkorir/driftguard) — completely different domain, no conflict
competitive_intelligence.litellm_supply_chain_note: LiteLLM versions 1.82.7-1.82.8 compromised March 24 2026. Used direct Anthropic SDK in drift-guard — correct call.

llm_contract.judge_abstraction: Direct Anthropic + OpenAI SDKs (avoided LiteLLM due to supply chain attack March 2026)
llm_contract.storage: SQLite — zero dependencies, self-hosted, user owns data

drift_guard.llm_abstraction: Direct Anthropic SDK (avoided LiteLLM due to supply chain attack March 2026)
drift_guard.diff_handling: Truncates at 8000 chars with notice — KU-013 (large diff handling) acknowledged, v0.2 fix

shared.ci_publishing: OIDC trusted publishing via pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish — no stored API tokens

prompt_lock.priority_1: GitHub repo + README — primary organic discovery
prompt_lock.priority_2: Dev.to: 'How to catch LLM regressions in CI before they hit production'

llm_contract.priority_1: GitHub repo + README — primary organic discovery
llm_contract.priority_2: Dev.to: 'Why your LLM functions need behavioral contracts (not just Pydantic)'
llm_contract.promptfoo_angle: Promptfoo acquired by OpenAI. llm-contract = provider-agnostic behavioral contracts for teams who don't want OpenAI lock-in.

drift_guard.priority_1: GitHub repo + README — primary organic discovery
drift_guard.priority_2: Dev.to: 'Your PR description is a contract. Is your code fulfilling it?'
drift_guard.priority_3: r/devops + r/programming — 'AI writes 42% of code; nobody checks if PRs do what they say'
drift_guard.coderabbit_angle: CodeRabbit charges $24-30/dev/month and still doesn't verify intent. drift-guard is free and does exactly that.
drift_guard.priority_4: Show HN — target 10-30 points. Angle: 'I built a tool that checks if your PR does what it says it does'
